popularStory.headline: BIO
popularStory.description:
BIO:  Seigneur de Montlheri and Bray, 1102; Seigneur de Cheveuse and la Ferte; Vicomte de Troyes.  Crusader; captured at Roma, 1102, while on the First Crusade.

** from http://fmg.ac/Projects/MedLand …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Milon I "le Grand" de Bray, II de Montlhéry, Vicomte de Troyes於1035年出生在法國皮卡迪奧易時，他的父親Guy de Montlhéry I26歲，母親Hodierna de Gometz-La-Ferte31歲。他和Litause de Troyes DE BLOIS至少有4個兒子和7個女兒。他於1096年被列為croisé。他去世於1102年享年67歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Milon I "le Grand" de Bray, II de Montlhéry, Vicomte de Troyes于1035年出生在法国皮卡迪奧易时，他的父亲Guy de Montlhéry I26岁，母亲Hodierna de Gometz-La-Ferte31岁。他和Litause de Troyes DE BLOIS至少有4个儿子和7个女儿。他于1096年被列为croisé。他去世于1102年享年67岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bray,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places in Berkshire and Devon. The former is probably named with Old French bray ‘marsh’, the latter from the Cornish element bre ‘hill’.
English: perhaps a topographic name from northern Middle English bra ‘steep (river) bank’ or ‘brow of a hill’, denoting someone who lived at such a place.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in Normandy or Picardy called Bray (Eure, Calvados, Aisne, Somme); see 6.

variants.name: Dubray
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Napoleon.
French: habitational name, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, for someone from any of various minor places called Bray (see Bray ).
Altered form of French   Dubreuil  . This surname is common among Native Americans, namely the Cheyenne River Sioux in SD.

variants.name: Braye
meanings: English (Sussex): from the Middle English female personal name Braye. Its origin is unknown but it might derive from Breton bregh ‘fine, brave’.

variants.name: Brais
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Aldea, Andre, Cecile, Fernande, Stephane.
French: altered form of   Bray  .

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Troyes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Swiss German Treyer, a cognate of 2 below and 3 below or a variant of Dreyer .

German: variant of Treuer (and, in North America, probably also an altered form of this), in the south a topographic name for someone who lived by a cattle track (Treyen, from Latin trivium ‘trail’), in the north an occupational name derived from Middle Low German troye ‘jerkin’ (compare 5 below). The surname Troyer is very rare in Germany.
Americanized form of Austrian German Trojer: topographic name derived from South German dialect troi ‘cattle track’, a cognate of 2 above.

variants.name: Dreyer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Erwin, Fritz, Johannes, Kurt, Baerbel, Bernhard, Christoph, Claus, Dieter, Ernst, Hedwig.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname derived from German drei ‘three’, Middle High German drī(e), with the addition of the suffix -er. This was the name of a medieval coin worth three hellers (see Heller ), and it is possible that the German surname may have been derived from this word. More probably, the nickname is derived from some other connection with the number three, too anecdotal to be even guessed at now.
North German and Scandinavian: occupational name for a turner of wood or bone, from an agent derivative of Middle Low German dreien, dregen ‘to turn’. See also Dressler .

nameMeanings.name: Milon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin a local name from the numerous places so called, a large number of which get their name from Old English mylentūn ‘settlement with a mill’. Others were originally named as ‘the middle settlement (of three)’, from Old English middel ‘middle’ + tūn ‘settlement’. The surname is most famous as that of the poet John Milton ( 1608–74 ), and the given name is sometimes bestowed in his honour. Its most illustrious bearer in recent times has been the economist Milton Friedman ( 1912–2006 ).

nameMeanings.name: "le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  ♂ ‘Profit’ or ‘sharp’.
2  ♀, ♂ ‘Upright’.

nameMeanings.name: Grand"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, common in Scotland, where it is the name of a famous clan. It is derived from a nickname meaning ‘large’ (Anglo-Norman grand). In the United States the name is sometimes bestowed in honour of the Civil War general and 18th president, Ulysses S. Grant ( 1822–85 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Milon I "le Grand" de Bray, II de Montlhéry, Vicomte de Troyes was born in 1035, in Oise, Picardie, France, his father, Guy de Montlhéry I, was 26 and his mother, Hodierna de Gometz-La-Ferte, was 31. He had at least 4 sons and 7 daughters with Litause de Troyes DE BLOIS. In 1096, at the age of 61, his occupation is listed as croisé. He died in 1102, at the age of 67.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Milon I "le Grand" de Bray, II de Montlhéry, Vicomte de Troyes est né en 1035, à Oise, Picardie, France, son père, Guy de Montlhéry I, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Hodierna de Gometz-La-Ferte, avait 31 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 7 filles avec Litause de Troyes DE BLOIS. En 1096, à l’âge de 61 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est croisé. Il est décédé en 1102, à l’âge de 67 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Milon I "le Grand" de Bray, II de Montlhéry, Vicomte de Troyes 1035 in Oise, Picardie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Guy de Montlhéry I 26 und seine Mutter Hodierna de Gometz-La-Ferte 31. Er hatte mit Litause de Troyes DE BLOIS mindestens 4 Söhne und 7 Töchter. 1096, als er 61 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als croisé aufgeführt. Er starb 1102 im Alter von 67 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Milon I "le Grand" de Bray, II de Montlhéry, Vicomte de Troyes è nato il nel 1035 nel Oise, Picardie, Francia, il suo padre, Guy de Montlhéry I, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Hodierna de Gometz-La-Ferte, aveva 31 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 7 figlie con Litause de Troyes DE BLOIS. Nel 1096, all'età di 61 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come croisé. Morì nel 1102, all'età di 67 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Milon I "le Grand" de Bray, II de Montlhéry, Vicomte de Troyesが1035年にフランスピカーディオイゼで生まれた時，父Guy de Montlhéry Iは26歳，母Hodierna de Gometz-La-Ferteは31歳であった。彼はLitause de Troyes DE BLOISとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と7人の娘をもうけた。1096年，61歳の時，職業はcroiséと記録されている。彼は1102年に67歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Milon I "le Grand" de Bray, II de Montlhéry, Vicomte de Troyes(이)가 1035년 프랑스 피카디 이스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Guy de Montlhéry I(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Hodierna de Gometz-La-Ferte(은)는 31세였다. 그는 Litause de Troyes DE BLOIS(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 1096년, 61세의 나이로 그의 직업은 croisé이었다. 그는 1102년 67세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Milon I "le Grand" de Bray, II de Montlhéry, Vicomte de Troyes nasceu em 1035, em Oise, Picardie, França, seu pai, Guy de Montlhéry I, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Hodierna de Gometz-La-Ferte, tinha 31 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 7 filhas com Litause de Troyes DE BLOIS. Em 1096, com 61 anos, sua profissão aparece como croisé. Ele faleceu em 1102, com 67 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Milon I "le Grand" de Bray, II de Montlhéry, Vicomte de Troyes родился в 1035 г. в местности оис, пикарди, Франция, его отцу по имени Guy de Montlhéry I было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Hodierna de Gometz-La-Ferte, 31. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 7 дочерей от Litause de Troyes DE BLOIS. В 1096 году, в возрасте 61 год,  он работал по профессии croisé. Он умер в 1102 г. в возрасте 67 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Milon I "le Grand" de Bray, II de Montlhéry, Vicomte de Troyes nació en 1035, en Oise, Picardie, Francia, su papá, Guy de Montlhéry I, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Hodierna de Gometz-La-Ferte, tenía 31 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 7 hijas con Litause de Troyes DE BLOIS. En 1096, a la edad de 61 años, su ocupación registrada era croisé. Murió en 1102, a la edad de 67 años.
